assessment.summary: A present, quantified global toll — millions of deaths a year already associated with resistant bacterial infections — that is worsening across most tracked pathogens and carries mounting economic cost.
assessment.timeframe: Already underway; resistance is rising across the majority of tracked pathogen-antibiotic combinations, with costs projected to climb steadily through 2035.

claims.text: Bacterial antimicrobial resistance (AMR) was estimated to be associated with more than 4.7 million deaths globally in 2021.
The total cost to treat resistant bacterial infections alone is predicted to reach US$ 412 billion annually up to 2035, with additional productivity losses of US$ 443 billion per year.

description: Bacteria, viruses, fungi, and parasites evolving resistance to the drugs used to treat them, driven by overuse and misuse of antimicrobials in humans, animals, and agriculture. Unlike a single pandemic pathogen, AMR is a gradual, ongoing erosion of the effectiveness of modern medicine — common infections, surgery, and cancer treatment all become more dangerous as resistance spreads.
name: Antimicrobial Resistance
